items.title: NASA, OPM Launch NASA Force to Recruit Top Talent for US Space Program - NASA
items.content_text: The U.S. Office of Personnel Management (OPM) and NASA announced NASA Force on Wednesday, a dedicated talent track within the US Tech Force initiative

items.title: NASA, JAXA to Cover HTV-X1 Spacecraft Departure from Space Station - NASA
items.content_text: On Thursday, March 5, flight controllers will use the space station’s Canadarm2 robotic arm to detach HTV-X1 from the Harmony module’s Earth-facing port on

items.title: 2026 News Releases - NASA
items.content_text: Sign up to receive NASA news releases and other information.

items.title: Japanese Spacecraft Ready for Departure, Crew Studies Biology and Advanced Tech - NASA
items.content_text: Expedition 74 is ready to send off a Japanese cargo spacecraft on Friday after four months attached to the International Space Station. The orbital residents also spent Wednesday conducting a wide array of biology investigations to keep humans healthy on and off the Earth and tested advanced technology to benefit crew operations.

items.title: Japan’s Cargo Spacecraft Nears Departure as Crew Works Spacewalk Preps, Science - NASA
items.content_text: Mission managers have given the go for a Japanese cargo spacecraft to depart the International Space Station at the end of the week. Meanwhile, the Expedition 74 crew is reviewing upcoming spacewalk procedures, packing a second cargo spacecraft for its departure, studying artificial intelligence tools, and conducting Earth observations.

items.title: US-French Satellite Takes Stock of World’s River Water
items.content_text: Rivers rise and fall throughout the year, but by how much? Perhaps less than previously thought, according to new data from the SWOT mission. Hidden riverbed contours are also emerging.

items.title: NASA’s Curiosity Rover Sees Martian ‘Spiderwebs’ Up Close
items.content_text: A hilly landscape that looks like spiderwebs when viewed from orbit holds clues to the history of water on ancient Mars.

items.title: NASA’s Perseverance Now Autonomously Pinpoints Its Location on Mars
items.content_text: Using the new Mars Global Localization algorithm, NASA’s Perseverance rover can now pinpoint its exact location without help from engineers on Earth.

items.title: Archival Data From NASA’s NEOWISE Tracks Star Turning Into Black Hole
items.content_text: Using archival data from the NEOWISE mission, astronomers have captured the rare moment a massive star skipped the supernova phase and collapsed directly into a black hole.

items.title: NASA’s Perseverance Rover Completes First AI-Planned Drive on Mars
items.content_text: In a first for planetary exploration, NASA utilized generative AI to map routes for the Perseverance rover, cutting planning time and increasing science returns.

items.title: NASA-ISRO Radar Mission Peers Through Clouds to See Mississippi River Delta
items.content_text: NASA’s NISAR mission reveals new details of the Mississippi River Delta’s forests and wetlands, proving radar’s power to monitor ecosystems regardless of weather.

items.title: NASA Analysis Shows La Niña Limited Sea Level Rise in 2025
items.content_text: A mild La Niña caused greater rainfall over the Amazon basin, which offset rising sea levels due to record warming of Earth’s oceans.

items.title: Networks Keeping NASA’s Artemis II Mission Connected
items.content_text: Artemis missions rely on both the Near Space Network, managed by NASA Goddard, and the Deep Space Network, managed by the Jet Propulsion Laboratory.

items.title: NASA, SpaceX Launch US-European Satellite to Monitor Earth’s Oceans
items.content_text: Sentinel-6B will extend a decades-long legacy of global sea level measurements that help forecast weather, protect coastal communities, and more.
